title: podman.socket fails with 'trigger-limit-hit'
body:
### Issue Description

I can't use podman API socket, as after a while the socket failed.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
systemctl --user status podman.socket
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (listening) since Wed 2025-11-12 19:06:53 CET; 3s ago
 Invocation: 51d43f895c4a4d3db45a0f567df35d36
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
     Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
```


### Describe the results you received


```
systemctl --user status podman.socket
× podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: failed (Result: trigger-limit-hit) since Wed 2025-11-12 19:07:09 CET; 2h 55min ago
   Duration: 15.535s
 Invocation: 51d43f895c4a4d3db45a0f567df35d36
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
     Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

Nov 12 19:06:53 magnolia systemd[6723]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia systemd[6723]: podman.socket: Trigger limit hit, refusing further activation.
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia systemd[6723]: podman.socket: Failed with result 'trigger-limit-hit'.
```
As a side note, I want to stress the point that **podman.service** has not been triggered by socket activation, no idea one.

### Describe the results you expected

The socket shall be still listening.



### podman info output

```yaml
% podman version
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Verify the socket is in right path:
```
ls -al /run/user/1000/podman
total 0
drwx------  2 gabx gabx  60 Nov 12 22:02 ./
drwx------ 20 gabx gabx 720 Nov 12 22:08 ../
srw-rw----  1 gabx gabx   0 Nov 12 22:02 podman.sock=
```



### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @gabx could you please retry with the latest release v5.7.0 ?

not yet, as version 5.7 is not already packaged for arch linux. I prefer to wait for the official upgrade, then I will try.
As a side note, I have another error:
```
gabx@magnolia ➤➤ ~/.config % systemctl --user status podman.socket
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; disabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (listening) since Mon 2025-11-17 14:27:38 CET; 42s ago
 Invocation: 28796024b4aa4813a92b7367be4d0234
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
     Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

Nov 17 14:27:38 magnolia systemd[955]: Listening on Podman API Socket.


gabx@magnolia ➤➤ ~/.config % podman run hello-world
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/00-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 81df7ff16254 done   |
Copying config 5dd467fce5 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
/etc/kata-containers/configuration.toml: file /usr/share/kata-containers/vmlinux.container does not exist
ERRO[0003] Removing container 1fabce16c87c41c684b7ba1c87aa852b7b1b629930f90bbcf14fba8748614b7d from runtime after creation failed
Error: OCI runtime error: /usr/bin/kata-runtime: /etc/kata-containers/configuration.toml: file /usr/share/kata-containers/vmlinux.container does not exist
gabx@magnolia ➤➤ ~/.config % systemctl --user status podman.socket


● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; disabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (listening) since Mon 2025-11-17 14:27:38 CET; 1min 44s ago
 Invocation: 28796024b4aa4813a92b7367be4d0234
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
     Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

Nov 17 14:27:38 magnolia systemd[955]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
```

all my podman issues where due to an old alias in one of my shell startup files which forced podman to use kata-runtime as OCI.
After the removal of the alias, everything works OK
